CORRECTED post-implementation via live e2e testing: memberPubkeys updates on invite, not accept, so a pending contact IS already_member and correctly renders disabled ('already in group'), same as any other member. User-confirmed 2026-07-14 to ship without a re-invite path; original 'stays selectable' premise was factually wrong about runtime behavior. Follow-up filed: invite-modal-picker-cannot-re-invite.

CORRECTED and inverted post-implementation: groups-invite-pending-contact-selectable.spec.ts rewritten to assert the verified-real behavior (pending contact becomes disabled 'already in group', cannot be re-submitted) rather than the original false premise. Directly run live against Docker relay, passes.

gate_runs.detail: All 27 epic-touched e2e spec files (25 migrated + 2 new) run directly against Docker/strfry relay, all pass. 4 real bugs found and fixed during verification: (1) invalid bech32 checksum in a pre-existing test npub fixture, latent since before this epic, surfaced by the new helper's decode step; (2) incorrect assumption that Playwright's selectOption cannot force-select a disabled <option> -- it can in this Chromium build, test hardened to rely on the app's own isSelectionValid guard instead; (3) Next.js static-export trailing-slash href mismatch in a new spec; (4) DD-4 product-decision contradiction -- memberPubkeys updates on invite not on accept, so 'pending stays selectable' was factually wrong; user-confirmed correction, AC-UX-4/AC-E2E-8 and the corresponding test rewritten. 2 additional live-run failures (groups-profile-reactive, groups-lazy-updates) confirmed as pre-existing flakiness via direct git-stash baseline comparison, unrelated to this epic.
